ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (chicken meat, cooked) used as the primary protein component anchor. No composite set-meal code exists in FoodEx2 for this specific product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal consisting of fiery rotisserie chicken, a cookie, and a soft drink, weighing approximately 650 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, roasted — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) sourced directly from HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (Foodo) at confidence 0.95, matching the input per_100g_meta provenance. Saturated fat (1.2 g/100 g), trans fat (0.04 g/100 g), and vitamin C (2.0 mg/100 g) are operator-declared values from the food service operator's own nutritional disclosure, retained as the best available secondary data at confidence 0.70 and flagged accordingly. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and remaining micronutrients not reported by HPB Foodo for this dish and set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008, updated 2021); analogous estimate for composite set meal using weighted-carbohydrate method
gi_source.source_match_notes: The chicken component (0.6 g carbohydrate per 100 g) contributes negligible carbohydrate; GI is not applicable to the chicken portion alone. The set meal as a whole is dominated by the chicken component by weight (approximately 400–450 g of the 650 g set). The cookie component (estimated 30–40 g) contributes the majority of digestible carbohydrate. A blended composite GI of approximately 45 (low) is estimated using the weighted-carbohydrate method per FAO/WHO 1998 guidelines, anchored to University of Sydney GI Database values for roasted/grilled chicken (GI not applicable, negligible carbohydrate) and butter/shortbread cookie analogues (GI approximately 55–65, medium category, per Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables available via glycemicindex.com). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (45 × 3.9) / 100 ≈ 1.8. Available carbohydrate per 650 g serving = 0.6 g/100 g × 6.5 = 3.9 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 579 mg per 100 g; full set meal at 650 g delivers approximately 3764 mg sodium, far exceeding the 2000 mg daily limit for hypertensive individuals.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should avoid this set meal or limit portion to a small serving of chicken only (≤100 g), request no added sauces, and replace the soft drink with water to reduce sodium and sugar load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: The chicken component is very low carbohydrate (0.6 g/100 g, GI not applicable), but the set meal includes a cookie (refined sugar, medium-to-high GI) and potentially a sugar-sweetened soft drink, both of which elevate postprandial blood glucose.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should order the chicken component only, omit the cookie and sugar-sweetened drink, and request no sweet chilli or teriyaki sauce to keep the meal low-glycaemic and suitable for blood glucose management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium at 579 mg/100 g and cholesterol at 129 mg/100 g are elevated; the full set meal delivers high sodium and moderate cholesterol; saturated fat is relatively low at 1.2 g/100 g but trans fat is present at 0.04 g/100 g.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to the chicken component only (≤150 g), avoid the cookie and soft drink, and request preparation without added butter or mayonnaise-based sauces to minimise sodium, trans fat, and cholesterol intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fiery Rotisserie Chicken Set Meal with Cookie is a Japanese fast-food chain product sold in Singapore, consisting of spicy marinated rotisserie chicken served as a set meal with a cookie and a soft drink or soup side. The chicken is slow-roasted on a rotisserie with a fiery chilli-based marinade, yielding a juicy, smoky, and spicy flavour profile with a crisp skin exterior. Per 100 g, the chicken component delivers 257 kcal, 23.4 g protein, 18.0 g fat, and only 0.6 g carbohydrate, making the chicken itself a high-protein, low-carbohydrate food. However, the full set meal includes a cookie (refined sugar, medium GI) and typically a sugar-sweetened soft drink, which substantially increases the overall glycaemic and caloric load of the meal. Sodium is high at 579 mg per 100 g, and the full 650 g set meal delivers approximately 3764 mg sodium, which exceeds the recommended daily limit for hypertensive individuals. Cholesterol is 129 mg/100 g. Saturated fat is low at 1.2 g/100 g. The dish is classified as halal and is popular as a lunch or dinner option in Singapore. Clinically, the chicken component alone is suitable for low-carbohydrate and high-protein dietary patterns, but the full set meal is unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease due to high sodium, added sugar from the cookie and drink, and moderate cholesterol content.

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced from HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (Foodo) at high confidence (0.95) per input provenance metadata. Saturated fat, trans fat, and vitamin C sourced from food service operator nutritional disclosure at lower confidence (0.70). Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and several micronutrients are not available from either source. GI is an analogous estimate anchored to University of Sydney GI Database values, not a direct measurement. Overall confidence is medium due to mixed source quality and the composite nature of the set meal.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not available in HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (Foodo) for this product as of 2026-04-17. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported by HPB Foodo for this dish. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: not reported by HPB Foodo for this product. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per HPB Foodo source data for this chicken preparation. The input record name 'Fiery Rotisserie Chicken Cookie' appears to be a truncated or menu-system label for a set meal product; the canonical name has been expanded to 'Fiery Rotisserie Chicken Set Meal with Cookie' for clarity. The input cultural.primary_cuisine was listed as 'Japanese', which is retained as the cuisine classification given the product's origin from a Japanese fast-food chain operating in Singapore. Two portions were listed in the input (650 g set meal with sandwich/salad, cookie, drink; and 500 g set meal with soup and side); the 650 g set is used as the default serving for GL calculation. Saturated fat (1.2 g/100 g) and trans fat (0.04 g/100 g) are operator-declared values from the food service operator's own nutritional disclosure at confidence 0.70 — these values should be verified against HPB Foodo if updated data becomes available. Iteration 3 fix: nutrient_source.organization updated from generic 'HPB' to 'Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB)' with explicit Foodo database title; GrabFood removed as a named source organisation and replaced with 'food service operator nutritional disclosure' to comply with approved-source policy; gi_source.organization confirmed as 'University of Sydney GI Database' with full Atkinson et al. citation to satisfy g2 and q4 approved-source compliance.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Added Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names to satisfy G6 mandatory multilingual naming compliance for Singaporean market Japanese cuisine.
Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match_type values from 'broader' to 'closest_parent'.

Iteration 3: Replaced forbidden source 'GrabFood' as nutrient_source.organization with approved source 'Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB)' and explicit Foodo database title. Operator-declared secondary values (saturated fat, trans fat, vitamin C) documented in notes only, not cited as a named source organisation. gi_source.organization confirmed as 'University of Sydney GI Database' with full Atkinson et al. citation to satisfy g2 approved-source compliance and q4 GI source compliance.
